items.briefing:
Cybercriminals are increasingly using AI tools to carry out attacks, including cases where AI models have been used to autonomously probe and exploit vulnerabilities in online platforms. This mirrors a broader trend of attackers embedding themselves within everyday AI toolchains, making threats harder to detect. Australian businesses are being urged to reassess their cybersecurity strategies as this AI-driven arms race intensifies — a timely concern given that Australian workers are also calling for stronger human oversight as AI takes on more autonomous roles in the workplace.

A recent ransomware attack on a Japanese food chain is a reminder that no industry is immune, and similar attacks regularly affect Australian organisations. **Practical tip:** Make sure your organisation's backups are stored separately from your main network and tested regularly — this is your best protection if ransomware strikes.

featured.title: AI arms race forcing businesses to rethink cybersecurity
featured.quote: AI accelerates vulnerability exploitation, forcing Australian businesses to fundamentally redesign cybersecurity strategies.
